Province.title: Province
Province.description: Represents an Angolan province, primarily used for geographical categorization of companies and posts.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Province entity.

name.description: The official name of the province (e.g., 'Luanda', 'Bengo').

description.description: A brief description of the province.

isActiveMarket.description: Indicates if this province currently has active market participation or opportunities.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Defines categories for marketplace posts, such as 'Logística' or 'Produção'.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

description.description: A description of what this category entails.

CompanyProfile.title: CompanyProfile
CompanyProfile.description: Represents a registered commercial entity (producer, transporter, buyer) within the C-Chain network.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CompanyProfile entity.

name.description: The commercial name of the company or entity.

nif.description: The Número de Identificação Fiscal (Tax Identification Number) of the company.

profileType.description: The type of role the company plays in the network (e.g., producer, transporter, buyer).

provinceId.description: Reference to Province where the company's head office is located. (Relationship: Province 1:N CompanyProfile)

description.description: A detailed description of the company's activities or profile.

commercialLicenseDocumentUrl.description: URL to the company's commercial license document (PDF/JPG format).

isEmailVerified.description: Indicates if the company's registered email address has been verified.

registrationStatus.description: The current status of the company's registration on the network (e.g., 'pending', 'approved', 'rejected').

isVerified.description: Indicates if the company has undergone additional verification, possibly through a paid service or audit.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the company profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the company profile was last updated.

avatarInitials.description: Two-letter initials for display as an avatar, typically derived from the company name.

avatarUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture.

Post.title: Post
Post.description: Represents a marketplace opportunity or request posted by a CompanyProfile.

companyProfileId.description: Reference to the CompanyProfile that created this post. (Relationship: CompanyProfile 1:N Post)

title.description: A concise title describing the opportunity or request.

description.description: A detailed description of the post, including specifications, deadlines, and conditions.

image.description: URL to the main image for the post.

imageHint.description: A hint for AI image search based on the post content.

value.description: The estimated value of the opportunity in Kwanza (Kz) or 'Sob consulta' if not fixed.

category.description: The category this post belongs to.

location.description: The province where the opportunity is located.

urgency.description: The level of urgency for the post ('Normal' or 'Alta').

status.description: The current status of the post (e.g., 'New', 'Audited', 'Completed', 'Expired').

likes.description: The total number of likes received for this post.

publishedAt.description: Timestamp when the post was published and became visible.

user.description: Denormalized name of the authoring company.

avatar.description: Denormalized avatar initials of the author.

authorAvatarUrl.description: Denormalized avatar URL of the author.

authorIsVerified.description: Denormalized flag indicating if the author's company profile is verified.

authorIsSubscriber.description: Denormalized flag indicating if the author has a premium subscription.

PostAudit.title: PostAudit
PostAudit.description: Stores the results of an AI-powered logistical risk assessment for a specific Post.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PostAudit entity.

postId.description: Reference to the Post that was audited. (Relationship: Post 1:N PostAudit)

analysisText.description: The detailed text output from the AI's risk analysis.

riskScore.description: A numerical score representing the assessed risk level (e.g., 0-100).

auditTimestamp.description: Timestamp when the AI audit was performed.

auditModel.description: The name or version of the AI model used for the audit (e.g., 'gemini-2.5-flash-preview-09-2025').

Conversation.title: Conversation
Conversation.description: Represents a private negotiation or messaging thread between two CompanyProfiles.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Conversation entity.

participant1CompanyProfileId.description: Reference to the first CompanyProfile participating in the conversation. (Relationship: CompanyProfile N:N Conversation)

participant2CompanyProfileId.description: Reference to the second CompanyProfile participating in the conversation. (Relationship: CompanyProfile N:N Conversation)

postId.description: Optional reference to the Post that initiated this conversation. (Relationship: Post 0:1 Conversation)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the conversation was initiated.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the conversation was last updated (e.g., new message).

lastMessageAt.description: Timestamp of the most recent message in the conversation.

Message.title: Message
Message.description: Represents an individual message exchanged within a Conversation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Message entity.

conversationId.description: Reference to the Conversation this message belongs to. (Relationship: Conversation 1:N Message)

senderCompanyProfileId.description: Reference to the CompanyProfile who sent this message. (Relationship: CompanyProfile 1:N Message)

content.description: The text content of the message.

sentAt.description: Timestamp when the message was sent.

isReadByRecipient.description: Indicates if the message has been read by the recipient.

PostLike.title: PostLike
PostLike.description: Records when a CompanyProfile 'likes' a Post, contributing to the post's popularity count.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PostLike entity.

companyProfileId.description: Reference to the CompanyProfile that liked the post. (Relationship: CompanyProfile N:N Post)

postId.description: Reference to the Post that was liked. (Relationship: CompanyProfile N:N Post)

likedAt.description: Timestamp when the post was liked.

FollowerLink.title: FollowerLink
FollowerLink.description: A lightweight document indicating a follow relationship. The document ID is the UID of the respective user.

followedAt.description: Timestamp when the follow action occurred.

definition.description: Collection for Angolan provinces, used for geographical categorization. Publicly readable by all users.
Collection for marketplace post categories. Publicly readable by all users.

Stores profiles for registered commercial entities. The {companyProfileId} must match the authenticated user's UID (request.auth.uid) for ownership. Publicly readable, but only the owner can write. Includes 'subscriptionPlan' and 'isVerified' fields for role-based access checks.

params.name: companyProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier for the CompanyProfile, which is also the UID of the owning user (request.auth.uid).

definition.description: A subcollection storing the profiles that a user is following. The {followedId} is the UID of the user being followed. Only the owner can manage their own 'following' list.

params.name: companyProfileId
params.description: The UID of the user who is performing the follow action.

params.name: followedId
params.description: The UID of the user being followed.

definition.description: A subcollection storing the profiles that are following this user. This is written to via a batch write when a user follows another, but not directly editable.

params.name: companyProfileId
params.description: The UID of the user being followed.

params.name: followerId
params.description: The UID of the user who is following.

definition.description: Main collection for marketplace opportunities/requests. All authenticated users can read. Only the owning CompanyProfile (ownerCompanyProfileId == request.auth.uid) can create, update, or delete. Includes denormalized 'companyProfileId' for authorization independence, and 'likesCount' for denormalized popularity.

params.name: postId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Post.

definition.description: Stores AI-powered logistical risk assessments for a specific Post. Publicly readable by all authenticated users as part of the Post details. Written only by a backend service account.

params.name: postId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Post.

params.name: auditId
params.description: The unique identifier for the PostAudit.

definition.description: Represents a private messaging thread between two CompanyProfiles. Includes denormalized 'members' map (e.g., {uid1: true, uid2: true}) for authorization independence, allowing direct checks for participation (`request.auth.uid in resource.data.members`). Only participants can read/write.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Conversation.

definition.description: Individual messages within a Conversation. Includes denormalized 'conversationMembers' map (copied from parent Conversation) for authorization independence. Only conversation participants can read/write. Sender must match 'request.auth.uid' for creation.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Conversation.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Message.

definition.description: Records when a CompanyProfile likes a Post. Stored as a subcollection under the liking CompanyProfile. The {companyProfileId} must match 'request.auth.uid'. The {postId} is used as the document ID. Only the owner can create/delete their own likes.

params.name: companyProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier for the liking CompanyProfile, which is also the UID of the owning user (request.auth.uid).

params.name: postId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Post that was liked (used as the document ID for the like).

definition.description: Dedicated collection for admin role assignments. Document existence (`exists(/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))`) grants administrative privileges across the application. Only readable by the admin user themselves.

params.name: userId
params.description: The UID of the user assigned the admin role.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed to adhere to the core principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC, QAPs, and Invariants. It prioritizes direct security rule evaluation without reliance on expensive and insecure hierarchical `get()` operations, ensuring scalability and debuggability. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**

1.  **`CompanyProfile` Ownership:** Each `CompanyProfile` document is placed directly under `/companyProfiles/{companyProfileId}`, where `companyProfileId` is explicitly tied to the `request.auth.uid` of the owning user. This makes ownership checks atomic (`request.auth.uid == companyProfileId`).
2.  **`Post` Ownership:** `Post` documents are stored in a top-level collection `/posts/{postId}`. Crucially, each `Post` document includes a `companyProfileId` field (which corresponds to the `request.auth.uid` of the owning user). This denormalization allows security rules to verify post ownership directly (`request.auth.uid == resource.data.companyProfileId`) without needing to fetch the parent `CompanyProfile` document.
3.  **`Conversation` Membership:** `Conversation` documents include a `members` map (e.g., `members: { uid1: true, uid2: true }`). This denormalizes the participating user IDs, allowing conversation access rules to check `request.auth.uid in resource.data.members` without any `get()` operations.
4.  **`Message` Membership:** `Message` documents, as a subcollection of `Conversation`, further denormalize the `conversationMembers` map (copied from their parent `Conversation` during creation). This is critical for authorization independence, enabling message access rules to check `request.auth.uid in resource.data.conversationMembers` directly, avoiding `get()` calls on the parent `Conversation` document. The `senderCompanyProfileId` also confirms the message sender's identity.
5.  **`PostLike` Ownership:** `PostLike` documents are stored under `/companyProfiles/{companyProfileId}/likes/{postId}`. This path inherently includes the `companyProfileId` (which is the `request.auth.uid` of the liking user), making authorization checks atomic (`request.auth.uid == companyProfileId`).

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters) Support:**

1.  **Global Marketplace Feed (`/posts`):** All authenticated users have read access to the `/posts` collection. This allows client-side queries to fetch and filter posts by `categoryId`, `locationProvinceId`, `urgency`, and `status` using `where` clauses (e.g., `db.collection('posts').where('status', '==', 'Audited')`). The security rule simply asserts `allow read: if request.auth != null;`, ensuring that the server does not implicitly filter results for security, thus upholding QAPs.
2.  **User-Specific Data:** Collections like `/companyProfiles/{companyProfileId}` (where `companyProfileId` is the user's UID) and `/companyProfiles/{companyProfileId}/likes/{postId}` inherently provide QAP-compliant list operations. Users can efficiently query their own data (`db.collection('companyProfiles').doc(request.auth.uid).get()` or `db.collection('companyProfiles').doc(request.auth.uid).collection('likes').get()`) without needing complex security rule filters.
3.  **User Conversations (`/conversations`):** The denormalized `members` map in `Conversation` documents enables QAP-compliant queries such as `db.collection('conversations').where('members.' + request.auth.uid, '==', true).get()`, allowing users to list only the conversations they are part of.

This architecture minimizes rule complexity, enhances security by design, and supports efficient data retrieval patterns required by the application's dynamic marketplace features.
